changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_no_public_ip to k8s_networkpolicy_no_public_ingress_ip for clarity
Changed k8s_ingress_no_public_access to k8s_ingress_no_public_ingress for specificity
review_notes: The control SC-25 'Thin Nodes' is applicable to Kubernetes as it aligns with minimizing exposure and access to critical infrastructure. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions but needed slight adjustments for clarity and specificity. The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls, focusing on reducing attack surfaces and ensuring least privilege access.
naming_improvements: Use 'no_public_ingress_ip' instead of 'no_public_ip' for network policies to specify ingress context
Use 'no_public_ingress' instead of 'no_public_access' for ingress resources to specify ingress context
title: Thin Nodes
step1_reasoning: The control SC-25 'Thin Nodes' focuses on minimizing exposure and access to critical infrastructure. In Kubernetes, this can be achieved by ensuring that network policies prevent public IP exposure, services do not use external IPs unnecessarily, RBAC is configured to avoid over-privileged roles, and ingress resources are configured to restrict public access. These checks align with the principle of reducing attack surfaces and ensuring least privilege access.
